subject: Statistics
title: Spam Filters and Naive Bayes
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Spam Filters and Naive Bayes.

hook.text: How does your email know that 'FREE VIAGRA' is spam but 'free meeting Friday' isn't?

buildup.text: Naive Bayes calculates the probability of spam given each word. Some words strongly signal spam.

discovery.text: It multiplies probabilities word by word, updating its belief with each new feature. Fast and effective.

twist.text: 'Naive' means it assumes words are independent — which is false. But the filter works shockingly well anyway.

climax.text: Naive Bayes was the foundation of email spam filtering. It turned Bayesian statistics into everyday technology.

punchline.text: A wrong assumption, brilliantly applied, filters billions of emails.

quiz.question: Why is the naive Bayes spam filter called 'naive'?
options: It assumes all words in an email are independent of each other — which is unrealistic but works
